meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Overcome No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome a lack of SaaS knowledge with this expert-led student checklist. Build your first real-world app using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the SaaS knowledge gap requires moving from theory to execution. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass boilerplate fatigue. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative feature development, allowing you to focus on shipping functional, portfolio-ready products instead of debugging environment configurations.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the student's greatest adversary. You have the ambition and the raw coding skills, but the chasm between a local script and a production-ready SaaS application feels insurmountable. You are paralyzed by the 'how'—how to handle authentication, how to integrate databases, and how to scale without breaking your sanity.

Relief is found in the starter kit. It is the architectural blueprint that transforms your chaotic ideas into a structured, professional codebase. By treating your first project not as a massive undertaking but as a series of modular milestones, you stop being a student and start being an AI Builder. We are going to bridge the gap between 'I know how to code' and 'I built a SaaS'.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Architecture
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, define the core value proposition. A starter kit works best when you have a clear boundary for your scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Generate a clean, modular project architecture for a productivity SaaS that includes user auth, a PostgreSQL database schema, and a Stripe integration layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project folder with modular architecture based on the generated schema'

steps.title: Configure Authentication & Security
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Use your starter kit to implement industry-standard auth so you aren't reinventing the wheel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a secure authentication flow using NextAuth.js or Clerk. Ensure the setup supports JWT tokens and protected API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement secure auth middleware and protect /dashboard routes'

steps.title: Database Schema Scaffolding
steps.description: Your data model is the heart of the app. Use the starter kit to define relationships before building the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a Prisma schema for a task management system. Include User, Task, and Subscription models with appropriate relational constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply Prisma schema and generate client to database'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Avoid the 'Big Bang' release. Build one feature at a time using Claude Code to ensure the code stays clean and documented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CRUD service layer for the Task model. Include error handling and data validation using Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create task service layer and link to dashboard API endpoints'

steps.title: Deployment & Production Readiness
steps.description: A SaaS isn't real until it's live. Prepare your project for Vercel or Railway deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate environment variable templates and a deployment configuration file optimized for Vercel and PostgreSQL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Validate production build and generate environment variable documentation'

pro_tips: Always favor 'Buy over Build' for infrastructure components like Auth or Payments; your time is better spent on the unique value of your app.
Use TypeScript interfaces for every API response; it prevents 90% of the runtime bugs that frustrate beginner developers.
Maintain a 'Changelog.md' from day one; it serves as your narrative for job interviews and portfolio showcases.
Keep your database queries in separate service files rather than inside your UI components to ensure clean separation of concerns.
When Claude Code gets stuck on a complex bug, ask it to 'explain the current state of the dependency graph' to identify hidden conflicts.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, push your code to GitHub, write a brief README explaining the problem you solved, and deploy it to a live domain to start collecting user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Don't let your ideas stay in the 'draft' phase. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
